description: appShellStore exports an object literal with set/update methods directly forwarded from _store. This means appShellStore.set(...) and appShellStore.update(...) are public API and callable from any module. The PROP-011 audit (effectful-isolation.test.ts) is a STATIC TEXT SCAN — it detects after-the-fact violations but does NOT prevent them at the type or runtime level. A new module can call appShellStore.set() and the only line of defense is the audit test. If a developer forgets to run the test, or if the audit is bypassed (e.g., dynamic dispatch, eval, indirect indexing like store['set'](v)), the violation slips through. The audit also does not detect calls in transitive dependencies. The textbook structural-integrity remediation (cited in FIND-205 line 28-29) was 'make the public store interface read-only by exporting subscribe only and exposing set through a token/capability passed at component init time' — this approach was not taken. The current approach achieves audit-passing but does not actually constrain the surface.
